claim_bullets.bullet_text: The Carpenter arrived early for his meeting with “Thing Software” and was shown to conference room 13. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272, may)
The Carpenter normally worked through personal referrals, but from time to time a recruiter would slip through his screen. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272, may)
Many companies use white‑boarding code as an excuse to have a technical conversation with a candidate, and The Carpenter felt this was an opportunity. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272, may)
The Carpenter was a JavaScript programmer, well‑known for meticulous attention to detail and love for hand‑crafted, exquisitely joined code. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272, may)
Despite his experience and industry longevity, the Carpenter did not mind being asked to demonstrate his resume claims. (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.261-272, may)

source_record_text: Interview with The Carpenter for a job at Thing Software.
